title: Daily Market Digest: May 14, 2026
subtitle: Wall Street Rallies, Tech Leads Gains on Strong Earnings and US-China Optimism

items.headline: Wall Street Extends Rally, Major Indices Hit New Highs Amid Positive Sentiment
items.summary: U.S. equity markets advanced broadly on Thursday, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite touching fresh intraday and closing record highs. The Dow Jones Industrial Average reclaimed the psychologically significant 50,000 level. Overall market sentiment was buoyed by strong corporate earnings, particularly from Cisco Systems, optimism surrounding the ongoing US-China diplomatic summit, and robust April retail sales data. Investors also showed increasing confidence that geopolitical tensions related to the Iran conflict would not escalate further, easing some risk-off sentiment. The market continues to be heavily influenced by advancements in artificial intelligence, with its impact broadening across various sectors.

items.statContext: Gained +0.7% to +0.9%, hitting a new all-time high.
Advanced +0.8% to +1.0%, also reaching a new record high.
Climbed +0.7% to +0.8%, crossing above the 50,000 mark.

items.headline: Cisco Systems Surges on Strong Earnings and AI Restructuring
items.summary: Cisco Systems surged between 14.03% and 15.5% after reporting better-than-expected third-quarter earnings and raising its full-year guidance. The networking giant also unveiled an AI-focused restructuring plan, including approximately 4,000 job cuts, to reallocate resources towards AI infrastructure and high-speed networking.

items.headline: POET Technologies Jumps on Strategic Optical Networking Agreement
items.summary: Shares of POET Technologies climbed significantly, gaining between 24% and 28%. This surge followed the announcement of a strategic optical networking agreement with Lumilens, which includes an initial $50 million purchase order for optical engines.

items.headline: Nvidia Rallies as US Clears AI Chip Sales to China
items.summary: Chipmaker Nvidia saw its shares rise between 3.2% and 4.2% after Reuters reported that the U.S. had cleared approximately 10 Chinese firms to purchase Nvidia's H200 artificial intelligence chip. This development lifted Nvidia's market valuation and contributed to the broader tech rally.

items.headline: Doximity Tumbles on Q4 Earnings Miss and Disappointing Guidance
items.summary: Doximity's stock declined significantly, falling between 23.09% and 26.1%. The healthcare professional network missed Wall Street's fourth-quarter earnings expectations and provided disappointing guidance for the upcoming period.

items.headline: Canadian Solar Shares Decline on Mixed Q1 and Weak Q2 Outlook
items.summary: Canadian Solar shares dropped between 14.41% and 15.16% after the company reported a mixed first-quarter performance and issued weaker-than-expected revenue guidance for the second quarter.

items.headline: April Retail Sales Up 0.5%, Import Prices Surge 1.9%
items.summary: U.S. retail sales rose 0.5% in April, largely in line with economists' estimates, though analysts noted part of the increase likely reflected higher inflation. Separately, U.S. import prices surged 1.9% in April, marking the largest increase in four years, signaling persistent inflationary pressures. Initial jobless claims moderately increased last week, yet the labor market continues to show resilience.

items.headline: Kevin Warsh Confirmed as Next Federal Reserve Chair
items.summary: The Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh as the next Federal Reserve Chair, succeeding Jerome Powell whose term ends on May 15. This leadership change comes amidst ongoing concerns about surging inflation and an unstable economy, potentially leading to increased market volatility as Warsh's policy stance on inflation is closely watched.

items.headline: US-China Summit Fosters Optimism, Leaders Agree on Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: High-level diplomatic discussions between the U.S. and China generated broadly positive market sentiment. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping reportedly agreed that the Strait of Hormuz should remain open, easing fears of supply disruption and stabilizing oil prices. China also reportedly agreed to buy 200 Boeing aircraft.

items.headline: Technology Sector Leads Market Gains, AI Investment Continues to Drive Growth
items.summary: The technology sector continued its leading performance, with the S&P 500 technology index rising 1%. Substantial investment by 'Big Tech' in artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure and next-generation data centers remains a primary driver for the U.S. stock market's record-setting performance throughout the year. The impact of AI is broadening across semiconductors, infrastructure, and even parts of the industrial economy.

items.headline: Earnings Season Reinforces AI-Led Market; Key Reports Today
items.summary: Corporate earnings reported this season have consistently reinforced the narrative of an AI-led market. Beyond Cisco, companies like StubHub Holdings, Viking Holdings, and Yeti Holdings also reported better-than-expected profits. Several other companies, including Applied Materials ($AMAT), Urban One ($UONEK), and KeyCorp ($KEY), are scheduled to report earnings today.
